stories.title: Mississippi State Stuns Oklahoma 6-0 — 9-Year WCWS Streak Ends (Tier 1)
stories.posting_window: 9:00 AM ET (shock/upset angle), 9:30 AM ET (streak angle)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Mississippi State just shut out No. 1 Oklahoma 6-0.

Delainey Everett — 13.1 career innings before this Super Regional — threw a complete-game shutout.

First time OU was blanked in 399 games. The 9-year WCWS streak is over. 🥎

#HailState #BoomerSooner #NCAASoftball
x_posts.posting_time: 9:00 AM ET (shock/upset angle), 9:30 AM ET (streak angle)

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Oklahoma hasn't missed the WCWS since 2015.

Until now.

Mississippi State makes their FIRST-EVER WCWS appearance by beating the most dominant program of the last decade.

This is one for the history books. 🥎

#HailState #NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball
x_posts.posting_time: 9:00 AM ET (shock/upset angle), 9:30 AM ET (streak angle)

facebook.long_form:
Mississippi State just did the unthinkable.

On Sunday afternoon in Norman, a team that nobody expected to be there — the Bulldogs, a program that had never once reached the Women's College World Series — shut out No. 1 Oklahoma 6-0 in Game 3 of the Super Regional. Just like that, a 9-year streak was over.

The pitcher who ended it: Delainey Everett, a junior who had thrown just 13.1 innings in her entire career before this Super Regional. She stepped into the biggest moment in Mississippi State softball history and responded with a complete-game shutout — three hits, three strikeouts, zero runs. Oklahoma hadn't been held scoreless in 399 games. The last time it happened was the 2019 WCWS.

Kinley Keller drove in three runs. Morgan Bernardini and Paige Ernstes hit home runs. And Kendall Wells, who came in one homer away from Megan Grant's all-time record, was hit in the jaw by a pitch in the third inning — and came back out to play in the fourth. Oklahoma's season ended 52-10.

The Bulldogs are headed to Devon Park for the first time. It's the best story in college softball right now.

What did you think of that result? Were you watching on Sunday?

---
facebook.image_caption: Mississippi State just ended Oklahoma's 9-year WCWS streak. Their first-ever Women's College World Series appearance. What a Sunday. 🥎
facebook.posting_time: 9:00 AM ET (shock/upset angle), 9:30 AM ET (streak angle)

stories.title: WCWS Field Is Set — Eight Teams Headed to Devon Park (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
The 2026 WCWS field is COMPLETE. Eight teams headed to Devon Park.

🥎 Texas Tech vs. Mississippi State — 12 PM
🥎 Texas vs. Tennessee — 2:30 PM
🥎 Alabama vs. UCLA — 7 PM
🥎 Nebraska vs. Arkansas — 9:30 PM

May 28. Oklahoma City. Let's go. 🔥

#NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball #WCWS

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Two programs making their FIRST-EVER WCWS appearance in 2026.

Arkansas. Mississippi State.

Texas goes for back-to-back. Texas Tech back as last year's finalist.

Megan Grant (40 HRs) at Devon Park.

Thursday. 🥎

#NCAASoftball #WCWS #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
The wait is over — the 2026 Women's College World Series field is complete, and Devon Park opens May 28.

After Sunday's three decisive Game 3s, the eight-team bracket is set. Here's how it breaks down for opening day on Thursday:

Texas Tech vs. Mississippi State at noon ET. Texas vs. Tennessee at 2:30 PM. Alabama vs. UCLA at 7:00 PM. Nebraska vs. Arkansas at 9:30 PM.

Two programs are making their first-ever WCWS appearance: Arkansas (who swept Duke in the Super Regional) and Mississippi State (who just shocked No. 1 Oklahoma). Texas enters as the defending national champion, going for back-to-back titles. Texas Tech is back for their second straight trip after finishing as last year's runners-up.

And then there's Megan Grant. The UCLA outfielder broke the all-time NCAA single-season home run record at 40 this season. She'll take the field at Devon Park and try to add a national title to the résumé.

The 2026 WCWS has everything — Cinderellas, dynasties, individual records, and four SEC programs in the field. We'll be covering every game.

Who are you picking to win it all? Let us know below.

---
facebook.image_caption: Eight teams. Four first-round matchups. Devon Park opens Thursday. The 2026 WCWS is going to be must-watch softball. 🥎

stories.title: Texas Tech 16, Florida 7 — Run Rule, Back-to-Back WCWS (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Texas Tech run-ruled Florida 16-7 in FIVE INNINGS.

Five HRs. First NCAA tournament game ever with both teams scoring 7+ in the first two innings.

Florida's first home mercy-rule loss in program history.

Red Raiders headed back to the WCWS. 🥎

#WreckEm #GoGators #NCAASoftball

facebook.long_form:
Texas Tech put on an offensive show for the ages on Sunday — and ended Florida's season in the most emphatic way possible.

The Red Raiders defeated Florida 16-7 in just five innings, hitting five home runs and forcing a run-rule ending in Game 3 of the Gainesville Super Regional. It was the first time in NCAA tournament history that both teams scored seven or more runs through the first two innings. The Gators absorbed their first home mercy-rule loss in program history.

Texas Tech had already defeated Florida 10-8 in Game 1, then lost Game 2 before winning the finale in dominant fashion. The 11th-seeded Red Raiders will head to Devon Park for the second straight year — last season they finished as national runners-up to Texas.

The series also produced one of the wildest subplots of the entire tournament. But on the field, Texas Tech was the better team when it mattered most — and now they're one of eight teams standing.

Did you watch this series? That was something else.

---
facebook.image_caption: Texas Tech: 16 runs, five home runs, run-rule victory over Florida in five innings. The Red Raiders are headed back to the WCWS. 🥎

stories.title: Texas 5, Arizona State 0 — Kavan Shuts It Down, Third Straight WCWS (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Teagan Kavan. 106 pitches. Complete-game shutout in a win-or-go-home Game 3.

Texas 5, Arizona State 0.

The defending national champions are going to their THIRD STRAIGHT WCWS.

Kavan vs. the field. Book it. 🔥

#HookEm #NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
When Texas needed its ace the most, Teagan Kavan delivered.

The defending national champion Longhorns defeated Arizona State 5-0 in Game 3 of the Austin Super Regional on Sunday. Kavan threw all 106 pitches of a complete-game shutout in her third start of the series — after Arizona State won Game 1 and put the season on the line.

Texas advances to the Women's College World Series for the third consecutive year. No team in the current era has won back-to-back WCWS titles recently, but the Longhorns are the defending champions and they've just shown why they belong in that conversation. Kavan is one of the best in the country, and she's going to Devon Park running on competitive fire.

Arizona State went 45-17 on the season — a program that briefly sent Texas to the brink. They gave the Longhorns everything they had. But when the game was on the line in a winner-take-all matchup, Kavan didn't give an inch.

Texas going for back-to-back. Who's picking them to do it?

---
facebook.image_caption: Texas returns to the WCWS for the third straight year. Kavan's shutout in Game 3 sent the defending champions back to Devon Park. 🥎

stories.title: Wells Ends Season at 39 HRs — Grant's Record Stands for Now (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Kendall Wells finishes the 2026 season with 39 home runs.

Megan Grant's record: 40. Still standing.

Wells was hit in the jaw by a pitch in Game 3 and STILL came back out to play.

A freshman. 39 HRs. She'll be back in 2027. 👀

#BoomerSooner #NCAASoftball #Softball

facebook.long_form:
Kendall Wells' remarkable freshman season is over — and she finishes with 39 home runs.

Megan Grant's all-time NCAA record of 40 stands. Wells came in one swing away from tying it, and a pitch to the jaw in the third inning of Game 3 added a painful exclamation point to an incredible but incomplete chase. Wells returned to the field in the fourth inning, catcher's gear and all, but Oklahoma couldn't come back against Mississippi State.

Wells ends the 2026 season as the second-greatest single-season home run hitter in NCAA history. She's a freshman. She'll be back.

What makes this story so compelling is what comes next. Grant's record is 40 — and it was set this season. Wells has already shown she can hit at that pace. The 2027 season will bring Wells back to Norman with a chance to start that chase again, with the added context of knowing exactly how close she came.

For now, Oklahoma's season ends at 52-10. The WCWS goes on without the Sooners for the first time since 2015.

Wells gave everything she had. And 39 is a number nobody will forget.

---
facebook.image_caption: 39 home runs. A freshman. Hit in the jaw and still came back to play. Wells' season is over, but her story is far from done. 🥎

stories.title: Mia Williams vs. Florida — The Hit-By-Pitch Saga (Tier 3)

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Mia Williams hit by pitch FIVE TIMES vs. her old team Florida.

Her dad — NBA legend Jason Williams — got ejected from the stands.

She hit .435 with 23 HRs and helped run-rule the Gators.

Tournament's main character. 🥎

#WreckEm #NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
Five times.

Five times, Florida pitchers hit Mia Williams with a pitch during the Super Regional series. Three of those came in Game 3 alone. Williams, who transferred from Florida after the 2024 season, essentially walked into enemy territory — back to Gainesville, back against her former program — and was plunked more often than any player you'll see in a tournament game this season.

Her father, NBA legend Jason Williams — known as "White Chocolate" from his Sacramento Kings days — was ejected from the stands after a confrontation with Florida fans. The moment was captured on video and spread across sports social media well beyond the softball world.

And yet, through all of it, Mia Williams kept playing. She hit .435 this season, slugged .908, and hit 23 home runs. She helped Texas Tech run-rule the Gators in five innings and punch a ticket to the WCWS.

Whether those hit-by-pitches were intentional or coincidental, Williams became the defining human story of Super Regional weekend. She's headed to Devon Park. The Florida series is over. And the softball world is absolutely going to be watching when Texas Tech takes the field May 28.

What did you make of the Mia Williams situation? Let us know.

---
facebook.image_caption: Five hit-by-pitches. Her dad ejected from the stands. Still hit .435 this season. Mia Williams became the tournament's main character this weekend. 🥎

stories.title: Two WCWS First-Timers — Arkansas and Mississippi State Make History (Tier 3)

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
Two programs in the 2026 WCWS have never been there before.

Arkansas. Mississippi State.

Both made it by beating programs nobody expected them to beat.

First-timers with nothing to lose at Devon Park.

May 28 can't come fast enough. 🥎

#HailState #WPS #NCAASoftball

facebook.long_form:
Two programs in this year's Women's College World Series have never stood on that stage before.

Arkansas made their first-ever WCWS appearance by sweeping Duke in the Super Regional — a remarkable achievement for a program that went 0-for-5 in Super Regionals under head coach Courtney Deifel before this year.

Mississippi State made theirs by doing the impossible — shutting out No. 1 Oklahoma 6-0 on Oklahoma's home field in Game 3. Never been there before. Never been expected to be there. Now they're headed to Devon Park.

Both teams are SEC programs, and both represent something bigger than just a tournament bracket entry: years of investment, near-misses, and a fanbase that's been waiting for this moment. Arkansas opens against Nebraska on Thursday night at 9:30 PM ET. Mississippi State plays Texas Tech at noon.

First-timers at the WCWS tend to play loose. They don't have the weight of expectation. And both of these teams proved they can beat anyone.

The 2026 WCWS just got a lot more interesting.

---

research.facts:
Two WCWS First-Timers — Arkansas and Mississippi State
**Decision:** FOLLOW UP — Arkansas first WCWS covered May 23 (Story 3). Mississippi State first WCWS covered above. Combined first-timer preview is a new angle.
**Sources:**
- NCAA.com: "Mississippi State clinches first-ever WCWS appearance in historic fashion" (May 24, 2026)
- Bracket analysis from multiple sources confirming two first-timers
**Key Facts:**
- Arkansas: first-ever WCWS (covered May 23) [HIGH]
- Mississippi State: first-ever WCWS [HIGH]
- Both are SEC programs [HIGH]
- SEC sends four teams: Alabama, Arkansas, Mississippi State, Tennessee [HIGH]
- Nebraska vs. Arkansas first-round matchup (Game 4, 9:30 PM, May 28) [HIGH]
- Mississippi State vs. Texas Tech first-round matchup (Game 1, 12:00 PM, May 28) [HIGH]
**Freshness:** May 24-25, 2026 — within 24 hours.
